compiled_state.title: Remove 5-sub-agent cap for orchestration
compiled_state.description:
## Context

DSO plugin users running orchestration skills (sprint, debug-everything, fix-bug) are constrained by a hard cap of 5 concurrent sub-agents — a blunt workaround for session usage limits that no longer applies now that billing is metered and process improvements (dependency graphs, file impact analysis) naturally bound parallelism. The cap forces unnecessary batching overhead (commit + push between batches) and slows sprint execution without preventing the actual risk: exhausting session usage quotas.

Architecture: All three orchestration skills delegate sub-agent dispatch through agent-batch-lifecycle.sh, the sole integration point. The OAuth endpoint returns five_hour.utilization and seven_day.utilization as floats (0.0-1.0). Null/missing fields are treated as 0% (fail-open).

## Success Criteria

1. A check-usage.sh script polls the Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint, caches results in a shared flock-protected file (~/.cache/claude/usage-cache.json, 5-min TTL, locked via separate usage-cache.lock file), and returns a throttle verdict as exit code: 0=unlimited, 1=throttled, 2=paused
2. When 5-hour utilization reaches 90% OR 7-day utilization reaches 95%, agent-batch-lifecycle.sh emits MAX_AGENTS: 1 and orchestration skills dispatch exactly 1 sub-agent per batch; subsequent batches proceed only after the prior agent returns
3. When 5-hour utilization reaches 95% OR 7-day utilization reaches 98%, agent-batch-lifecycle.sh emits MAX_AGENTS: 0 and orchestration skills skip sub-agent dispatch, writing a ticket comment with utilization percentages and reset time
4. An orchestration.max_agents config key in dso-config.conf caps the upper bound on concurrent sub-agents; when unset and verdict is unlimited, MAX_AGENTS: unlimited is emitted with no artificial ceiling — verified by test dispatching 10 agents without truncation
5. CLAUDE.md rule #4 no longer contains the phrase "5 sub-agents"; it references the orchestration.max_agents config key and the two throttle tiers — verified by grep returning no matches
6. An error-reactive fallback pattern-matches sub-agent result text for rate-limit keywords (rate.limit|usage.limit|quota.exceeded) and triggers throttling to 1 agent when matched — verified by test injecting error string and asserting throttle activates
7. Test asserts that with verdict unlimited and max_agents unset, a batch of 10 independent stories dispatches 10 concurrent sub-agents with no intervening commit/push steps

## Integration Details

- CI fallback: no credentials -> exit 0 (unlimited), USAGE_SOURCE: no-credentials
- Linux token: ~/.claude/.credentials.json -> claudeAiOauth.accessToken key path
- Portable locking: Python fcntl.flock (not flock(1) CLI)
- 429 stale-cache: use last known verdict (fail-closed toward throttled); cold-cache 429: exit 1 (throttled to 1)
- In-flight agents complete when throttle transitions mid-sprint
- Cache TTL floor: 60s minimum enforced regardless of config
- Cache dir: mkdir -p ~/.cache/claude/ before write

## Approach

agent-batch-lifecycle.sh is extended: its cmd_pre_check() calls check-usage.sh and emits MAX_AGENTS: unlimited|<N>|0 (replacing the current binary 5/1 output). check-usage.sh handles OAuth polling, caching, and CI fallback. An error-reactive wrapper in agent-batch-lifecycle.sh pattern-matches sub-agent result text for rate-limit errors as a secondary signal.

## Scenario Analysis

- flock on deleted cache file (high): Lock on separate lockfile + atomic rename for writes
- OAuth endpoint timeout (critical): curl --max-time 8 enforced
- Error-reactive false positives (medium): Match quota-specific keywords only, not generic HTTP 429
- Null API fields (high): Treat as 0% utilization (fail-open)
- Missing cache dir on first run (medium): mkdir -p before write
- CI runners have no credentials (high): Short-circuit to unlimited
- flock portability on macOS (high): Python fcntl.flock for portability
- Cache TTL of 0 (medium): Floor at 60s enforced
- Multiple OAuth tokens (high): Use claudeAiOauth.accessToken key path
- Immediate pause on first run (high): Log warning with reset time and suggest manual override

## Dependencies

- Coordinate with dso-0wi2 (config flags): ensure orchestration.max_agents follows config extension pattern
- Coordinate with 5df7-63ac (observability): this epic owns throttling only, not analytics/telemetry
- Coordinate with dso-9fgn (limit tool use): that epic constrains tools within agents; this constrains how many agents are dispatched

## Spike Recommendation

~2 days before implementation: (1) verify Linux credentials path yields valid OAuth token, (2) confirm endpoint response schema under 429 conditions, (3) validate Python fcntl.flock under concurrent access from two shell sessions

## Research Findings

### External Integration: Anthropic OAuth Usage Endpoint
- Trigger: External integration
- Query: OAuth usage endpoint behavior and rate limiting
- Sources: github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/31021, github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/31637
- Key insight: Endpoint rate-limits aggressively (~1 call/min, 429s persist for hours with no Retry-After header). 5-min cache TTL + flock prevents multi-session hammering.

### Prior Art: Usage-Aware Throttling
- Trigger: External integration
- Query: Claude Code plugins implementing usage monitoring
- Sources: github.com/mironmax/claudecode-plugins, github.com/aryansidbatte/nice-claude-status-bar, github.com/wntdev99/claude-code-server
- Key insight: mironmax uses PostToolUse hook with 60s cache and 95%/98% thresholds. wntdev99 uses TokenTracker + IntelligentThrottler at 85% warn / 90% pause. Our approach follows the mironmax pattern but with 5-min cache to avoid 429s.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration, security/credentials
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 10
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
